posts.name: Massimiliano Galli
posts.text:
Dear all,
I need to change the role of a process from signal to background in a very large datacard (i.e. the number assigned to it that combine uses to distinguish between the two). I remember that there should be a function for it in CombineHarvester, but I can’t find it at the moment. Could anyone please point me to an example that I can use?
Thank you,
Massimiliano
posts.cooked_html:
Dear all,
I need to change the role of a process from signal to background in a very large datacard (i.e. the number assigned to it that combine uses to distinguish between the two). I remember that there should be a function for it in CombineHarvester, but I can’t find it at the moment. Could anyone please point me to an example that I can use?

Thank you,
Massimiliano

posts.name: Marcel Rieger
posts.text:
Hi @gallim ,
in case using simple shell instructions is also an option, the following would do the trick.
If your signal is “-10” and should be changed to “42”, do
src="-10"
dst="42"

sed -r "$( sed -n -r '/^process\s+-?[0-9]/=' datacard.txt )s/\s${src}\s/ ${dst} /g" datacard.txt

(the inner/first sed looks up the number of the process numbering line, the outer/second sed is doing the replacement only in that line)
This stdout’s the new datacard. Add -i to the outer sed to make this in-place.
Hope this helps,
Marcel

posts.name: Adinda de Wit
posts.text:
Hi Massimiliano,
I think you’re looking for the set_signal function: https://github.com/cms-analysis/CombineHarvester/blob/0f22c7b157c9aef62a593a80a092319eea38d523/docs/PythonInterface.md?plain=1#L166 (but you want true->false)
Best,
Adinda
